bead.title: server: Artifacts GraphQL resolver returns HTTP 500 — guard nil artifact-type index and stop resolver panics surfacing as 500
bead.description:
PROBLEM: Browsing the Artifacts page in the ddx-server web UI returns HTTP 500 (observed 2026-05-27). A 500 (rather than a GraphQL error inside a 200 body) means something in the artifacts resolver chain PANICS and the HTTP layer converts it to 500.

ROOT CAUSE (identified candidate): cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifact_types.go:44-48 — attachArtifactTypeDefinitionsToArtifacts() loops project roots calling artifactTypeLoader.Load(root); it checks the returned err but does NOT check whether `index` is nil, then dereferences `index.Types` at line 48. A nil index (now, or after a future loader change) panics with a nil-pointer dereference. attachArtifactTypeDefinitionsToArtifacts is invoked from the Artifacts() query resolver at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go:184 (resolver entry at resolver_artifacts.go:92). More generally, any panic anywhere in the Artifacts() chain becomes a 500 instead of a structured GraphQL error.

PROPOSED FIX:
1. Reproduce the actual 500: issue the `artifacts(projectID: ...)` GraphQL query (see frontend query in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts) against a registered project and capture the panic/stack from the server.
2. Add a nil-index guard after Load() in resolver_artifact_types.go (return a wrapped error, never dereference a nil index).
3. Fix the specific panic the repro surfaces, and confirm the gqlgen panic-recovery path returns a structured error (HTTP 200 + errors[]) rather than 500.

NON-SCOPE: frontend changes; the Documents resolver; artifact content rendering.
bead.acceptance:
1. resolver_artifact_types.go guards a nil index returned by artifactTypeLoader.Load and returns an error instead of dereferencing index.Types.
2. New test in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifact_types_test.go (e.g. TestAttachArtifactTypeDefinitions_NilIndexReturnsError) asserts a nil-index path returns an error and does NOT panic.
3. New/extended test in cli/internal/server/graphql/artifacts_test.go exercises Artifacts() end-to-end against a project with NO artifact-type definitions and asserts success (no panic / no 500).
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... green
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes
